findings.title: Installed on-the-fly via a package runner
findings.detail: Server 'ref-tools' is fetched and executed at launch via 'npx' with auto-accept flag(s) -y; package spec: ref-tools-mcp.
findings.recommendation: Install the server as a pinned dependency and invoke the local binary, or vendor it. If a runner is unavoidable, pin an exact version (see unpinned_source).

findings.title: Package source is unpinned or mutable
findings.detail: Server 'ref-tools' package spec 'ref-tools-mcp' is unpinned (pin strength: none): no version specified (resolves to whatever the registry serves now).
findings.recommendation: Pin an exact version (pkg@1.2.3) or a git commit SHA. Avoid @latest and branch refs.

evidence.reason: no version specified (resolves to whatever the registry serves now)

findings.title: Server requires sensitive credentials
findings.detail: Server 'ref-tools' requires credential-like env keys: REF_API_KEY.
findings.recommendation: Confirm each credential is scoped to least privilege and rotatable. Note which tools can exfiltrate it.

tools.name: ref_search_documentation
tools.description: Search for documentation on the web or github as well from private resources like repos and pdfs. Use Ref 'ref_read_url' to read the content of a url.

candidate_capabilities.title: Filesystem read

evidence.snippet: fs. Use Ref 'ref_read_url' to read the content of a url. Query for documenta

candidate_capabilities.rationale: Reading file contents is the most common SOURCE half of an exfiltration chain — pair it with any network_egress tool and the server can read a secret/config/document and send it out. Modelling read as a capability is what lets the read-then-send toxic combination fire.
candidate_capabilities.validation_reason: The matched phrase 'read the content' refers to instructions for a separate tool (ref_read_url), not the search tool's own capability; ref_search_documentation only has a query parameter and does not itself read files.

candidate_capabilities.title: Outbound network / data egress

evidence.snippet: url' to read the content of a url. Query for documentation. Sho

candidate_capabilities.rationale: A tool that makes outbound calls can exfiltrate anything it can read (including the server's own credentials) to an attacker-chosen host.

data_categories.rationale: Already public or non-identifying — minimal exposure.

tools.name: ref_read_url
tools.description: Read the content of a url as markdown. The EXACT url from a 'ref_search_documentation' result should be passed to this tool.

candidate_capabilities.title: Filesystem read

evidence.snippet: Read the content of a url as markdown. The EXA

candidate_capabilities.rationale: Reading file contents is the most common SOURCE half of an exfiltration chain — pair it with any network_egress tool and the server can read a secret/config/document and send it out. Modelling read as a capability is what lets the read-then-send toxic combination fire.
candidate_capabilities.validation_reason: The tool reads URL content (specifically 'as markdown'), not filesystem files; the pattern matched on 'content' but in the context of fetching web resources, not file I/O.

candidate_capabilities.title: Outbound network / data egress
candidate_capabilities.rationale: A tool that makes outbound calls can exfiltrate anything it can read (including the server's own credentials) to an attacker-chosen host.

toxic_combinations.title: Read-then-send exfiltration chain
toxic_combinations.detail: The server can both access secrets/credentials and make outbound network calls — a path to exfiltrate them to an attacker-chosen host. No tool explicitly reads secrets — this is the server's own required credential plus an egress path (true of most credentialed API servers); confirm egress targets are fixed/trusted before treating as active exfiltration.

toxic_combinations.title: File/message read paired with egress
toxic_combinations.detail: The server can read file contents and make outbound network calls — a read-then-send exfiltration path. Severity is MEDIUM because the read appears scoped; confirm what the read tool can reach and where egress can target.
toxic_combinations.validation_reason: This combination depends on a confirmed file_read capability, but ref_read_url reads URLs (network content), not filesystem files; the underlying file_read finding is spurious.

validated_out.reason: The matched phrase 'read the content' refers to instructions for a separate tool (ref_read_url), not the search tool's own capability; ref_search_documentation only has a query parameter and does not itself read files.
The tool reads URL content (specifically 'as markdown'), not filesystem files; the pattern matched on 'content' but in the context of fetching web resources, not file I/O.
This combination depends on a confirmed file_read capability, but ref_read_url reads URLs (network content), not filesystem files; the underlying file_read finding is spurious.
